pages.raw_text:
The Metal Trades department of
the American Federation of Labor
has formed a permanent organlza-
ton with close to 100000 men af-
filiated. composed of machinists,
molders, boilermakers, metal pol
ishers and brass workers, pattern
makers, blacksmiths, foundry la
borers, stove mounters, core
makers. sheet metal workers and
electrical workers. 1t is probable
that the bridge and structural iron
workers and the iron and steel
(mill) workers will also join the
department. Jas. O'Donnell OF the
machinists, was elected president;
Jos. w. Valentine of the molders;
A. B. Grout of the metal polisners,
]. w. Kline of the blacksmiths, and
Geo. F. Dunn of the boilermakers,
vice presidents Jas. l.. Gernon Is
secretary-treasurer Headquarters
of the department will be in Wash
ington.


The eight-houi law Of Wisconsin
was upheld in the lower courts in
test case brought by a railway
corporation which company was
fined $1,000 for employing tele-
grapher more than eight hours pei
day at a way station. The case
will be appealed to the state SUI
preme court and ultimately to the
United States supreme court, as
interstate commerce laws are in
volved. The litigation is being
watched with great interest by or
ganized labor as well as by corpor
otion off rialg
